metadata.title:
GOLDEN KEY or SPOKANE


GIVEN QUEEN ELIZABETH

pages.raw_text:
1t appears to be one Of those ques-
tions that must be tried out In the
great crucible Of practical expert
ence-the friction Of agitation, of
give and take, of success and failure
JUST as all progress Of whatsoever
kind Is made. During the past two
or three decades labor has discover
ed that its greatest success has been
along the line OF organized effort on
the part of those of kindred inter-
ests; hence the labor unions. And,
my friends, the wonderful success
Of the unions in the lettering Of the
conditions Of the wage Garner is
sure guarantee that the union 10 here
TO stay. That explains why between
five and six thousand wage earners
of Spokane are represented here to-
night, actuated by common pur-
pose. There is a bond Of union and
fealty pervading the entire ranks of
organized labor. And it Is a senti-
ment Of interdependence that Is bound
to grow and crystalize into greater
benefits as time advances. And why
should not the uneducated wage earn
er organize? Do not his educated
brethren Of the legal. medical and
other fraternities set the example?
If you want a case tried in court, 1t
must be handled by a union lawyer
before a union Judge. If you are
sick. and are wise. you will be treat
ed by a union physician. These fra
ternities have found organization is
essential to their success, and while
they style themselves the bar, So
ciety or business association. the
union priciple Is there nevertheless.
The business men, recognizing the
power to be derived from united eff
fort, have organized for the advance
ment Of the business interests of the
city. 1t is styled the chamber of
commerce. Actuated by like motives,
organized labor has instituted this
Trades council and are ambitious of
making 1t of equal Importance in
the city's welfare.


cit Is true that at times SOME
unions are somewhat unreasonable
and guilty of injustice. But so are
many trusts and corporations badly
managed and viciously unjust in their
treatment of the public. The labor
union is not yet perfect. but 1t will
improve as its members become more
enlightened through experience, study
and fraternal intercourse. The union
stands for all that Is best In its
particular craft. Tho craftsman who
does not belong to his union, may
every time. be put down ~ morally
wrong" mentally weak and deficient
In skill. The union stands for the
best manhood, the best skill, the best
oitixsuship, the highest intelligence,
in the ranfs Of labor. The union man
loves his home. his union, his town,
his country, and win night for his nag.
Can you ask anything more of organ
lied labor?"
